}\n}\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhen no routing match is found, the global provider remains the fallback.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eNote:\u003c/strong\u003e \u003ccode\u003eapi_key\u003c/code\u003e values in \u003ccode\u003esettings.json\u003c/code\u003e are stored in plaintext. Keep this file private and do not commit it to version control.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eWeb Search and Fetch\u003c/h2\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-web-search-and-fetch\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Web Search and Fetch\" href=\"#web-search-and-fetch\"\u003e\u003csvg class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eBy default, \u003ccode\u003eWebSearch\u003c/code\u003e works on non-Anthropic models using DuckDuckGo. This gives GPT-4o, DeepSeek, Gemini, Ollama, and other OpenAI-compatible providers a free web search path out of the box.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eNote:\u003c/strong\u003e DuckDuckGo fallback works by scraping search results and may be rate-limited, blocked, or subject to DuckDuckGo's Terms of Service. If you want a more reliable supported option, configure Firecrawl.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eFor Anthropic-native backends and Codex responses, OpenClaude keeps the native provider web search behavior.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eWebFetch\u003c/code\u003e works, but its basic HTTP plus HTML-to-markdown path can still fail on JavaScript-rendered sites or sites that block plain HTTP requests.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eSet a \u003ca href=\"https://firecrawl.dev\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eFirecrawl\u003c/a\u003e API key if you want Firecrawl-powered search/fetch behavior:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"export FIRECRAWL_API_KEY=your-key-here\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003eexport\u003c/span\u003e FIRECRAWL_API_KEY=your-key-here\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eWith Firecrawl enabled:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003eWebSearch\u003c/code\u003e can use Firecrawl's search API while DuckDuckGo remains the default free path for non-Claude models\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003eWebFetch\u003c/code\u003e uses Firecrawl's scrape endpoint instead of raw HTTP, handling JS-rendered pages correctly\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eFree tier at \u003ca href=\"https://firecrawl.dev\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003efirecrawl.dev\u003c/a\u003e includes 500 credits. The key is optional.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003chr\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eHeadless gRPC Server\u003c/h2\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-headless-grpc-server\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Headless gRPC Server\" href=\"#headless-grpc-server\"\u003e\u003csvg class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eOpenClaude can be run as a headless gRPC service, allowing you to integrate its agentic capabilities (tools, bash, file editing) into other applications, CI/CD pipelines, or custom user interfaces. The server uses bidirectional streaming to send real-time text chunks, tool calls, and request permissions for sensitive commands.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e1. It acts just like the main interactive CLI, rendering colors, streaming tokens, and prompting you for tool permissions (y/n) via the gRPC \u003ccode\u003eaction_required\u003c/code\u003e event.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIn a separate terminal, run:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"npm run dev:grpc:cli\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003enpm run dev:grpc:cli\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cem\u003eNote: The gRPC definitions are located in \u003ccode\u003esrc/proto/openclaude.proto\u003c/code\u003e. You can use this file to generate clients in Python, Go, Rust, or any other language.\u003c/em\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003chr\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 test\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003ebun run test:coverage\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003ebun run security:pr-scan -- --base origin/main\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003ebun run smoke\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003ebun run doctor:runtime\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003ebun run verify:privacy\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003efocused \u003ccode\u003ebun test dir=\"auto\"\u003eOpenClaude uses Bun's built-in test runner for unit tests.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eRun the full unit suite:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"bun test\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003ebun \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003etest\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eGenerate unit test coverage:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"bun run test:coverage\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003ebun run test:coverage\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eOpen the visual coverage report:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"open coverage/index.html\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003eopen coverage/index.html\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIf you already have \u003ccode\u003ecoverage/lcov.info\u003c/code\u003e and only want to rebuild the UI:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"bun run test:coverage:ui\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003ebun run test:coverage:ui\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eUse focused test runs when you only touch one area:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003ebun run test:provider\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003ebun run test:provider-recommendation\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003ebun test path/to/file.test.ts\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eRecommended contributor validation before opening a PR:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003ebun run build\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003ebun run smoke\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003ebun run test:coverage\u003c/code\u003e for broader unit coverage when your change affects shared runtime or provider logic\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003efocused \u003ccode\u003ebun test ...\u003c/code\u003e runs for the files and flows you changed\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eCoverage output is written to \u003ccode\u003ecoverage/lcov.info\u003c/code\u003e, and OpenClaude also generates a git-activity-style heatmap at \u003ccode\u003ecoverage/index.html\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" data-discover="true">scripts</a></div></div></div></div></td><td class="react-directory-row-commit-cell"><div><div class="react-directory-commit-message"><a data-pjax="true" title="feat: add headless gRPC server for external agent integration (#278)

* gRPC Server

* gRPC fix

* UpdProto

* fix: address PR review feedback for gRPC server

- Update bun.lock for new dependencies (frozen-lockfile CI fix)
- Add multi-turn session persistence via initialMessages
- Replace hardcoded done payload with real token counts
- Default bind to localhost instead of 0.0.0.0

* fix(grpc): startup parity, cancel interrupt, and cli text fallback

- Replace enableConfigs() with await init() in start-grpc.ts for full
  bootstrap parity with the main CLI (env vars, CA certs, mTLS, proxy,
  OAuth, Windows shell)
- Call engine.interrupt() before call.end() in the cancel handler so
  in-flight model/tool execution is actually stopped
- Show done.full_text in the CLI client when no text_chunk was received,
  preventing silent drops when streaming is unavailable

* fix(grpc): wire session_id end-to-end and remove dead provider field

- Move session_id from ClientMessage into ChatRequest to fix proto-loader
  oneofs encoding bug and make the field functional
- Implement in-memory session store so reconnecting with the same
  session_id resumes conversation context across streams
- Remove ChatRequest.provider — per-request provider routing requires
  global process.env mutation, unsafe for concurrent clients; provider
  is configured via env vars at server startup

* fix(grpc): mirror CLI auth bootstrap in start-grpc and fix tool_name field

scripts/start-grpc.ts now runs the same provider/auth bootstrap as the
normal CLI entrypoint: enableConfigs, safe env vars, Gemini/GitHub token
hydration, saved-profile resolution with warn-and-fallback, and provider
validation before the server binds.

ToolCallResult.tool_name was being populated with the tool_use_id UUID.
Added a toolNameById map (filled in canUseTool) so tool_name now carries
the actual tool name (e.g. "Bash"). The UUID moves to a new tool_use_id
field (proto field 4) for client-side correlation.

* fix(grpc): add tool_use_id to ToolCallStart and interrupt engine on stream close

Two blocker-level issues flagged in code review:

- ToolCallStart was missing tool_use_id, making it impossible for clients
  to correlate tool_start events with tool_result when the same tool runs
  multiple times. Added tool_use_id = 3 to the proto message and populated
  it from the toolUseID parameter in canUseTool.

- On stream close without an explicit CancelSignal the server only nulled
  the engine reference, leaving the underlying model/tool work running
  as an orphan. Added engine.interrupt() in the call.on('end') handler
  to stop work immediately when the client disconnects.

* fix(grpc): resolve pending promises on disconnect and guard post-cancel writes

Four lifecycle and contract issues identified during proactive review:

- Pending permission Promises in canUseTool would hang forever if the
  client disconnected mid-stream. On call 'end', all pending resolvers
  are now called with 'no' so the engine can unblock and terminate.

- The done message and session save could fire after call.end() when
  a CancelSignal arrived mid-generation. Added an `interrupted` flag
  set on both cancel and stream close to gate all post-loop writes.

- The session map had no eviction policy, allowing unbounded memory
  growth. Capped at MAX_SESSIONS=1000 with FIFO eviction of the
  oldest entry.

- Field 3 was silently absent from ChatRequest. Added `reserved 3`
  to document the gap and prevent accidental reuse in future.

* fix(grpc): reset previousMessages on each new request to prevent session history leak

previousMessages was declared at stream scope and only overwritten when
the incoming session_id already existed in the session store. A second
request on the same stream with a new session_id would silently inherit
the first request's conversation history in initialMessages instead of
starting fresh, violating the session contract.

Fix: reset previousMessages to [] at the start of each ChatRequest
before the session-store lookup.

* fix(grpc): reset interrupted flag between requests and guard against concurrent ChatRequest

Two stream-scoped state bugs found during proactive audit:

- The `interrupted` flag was never reset between requests on the same
  stream. If the first request was cancelled, all subsequent requests
  would silently skip the done message, causing the client to hang.

- A second ChatRequest arriving while the first was still processing
  would overwrite the engine reference, corrupting the lifecycle of
  both requests. Now returns ALREADY_EXISTS error instead. Engine is
  nulled after the for-await loop completes so subsequent requests
  can proceed normally.

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>" class="Link--secondary" href="/Gitlawb/openclaude/commit/26eef92fe72e9c3958d61435b8d3571e12bf2b74">feat: add headless gRPC server for external agent integration (</a><a class="issue-link js-issue-link" data-error-text="Failed to load title" data-id="4200935893" data-permission-text="Title is private" data-url="https://github.com/Gitlawb/openclaude/issues/278" data-hovercard-type="pull_request" data-hovercard-url="/Gitlawb/openclaude/pull/278/hovercard" href="https://github.com/Gitlawb/openclaude/pull/278" aria-keyshortcuts="Alt+ArrowUp">#278</a><a data-pjax="true" title="feat: add headless gRPC server for external agent integration (#278)

data-discover="true">.gitignore</a></div></div></div></div></td><td class="react-directory-row-commit-cell"><div><div class="react-directory-commit-message"><a data-pjax="true" title="feat: add headless gRPC server for external agent integration (#278)

* gRPC Server

* gRPC fix

* UpdProto

* fix: address PR review feedback for gRPC server

- Update bun.lock for new dependencies (frozen-lockfile CI fix)
- Add multi-turn session persistence via initialMessages
- Replace hardcoded done payload with real token counts
- Default bind to localhost instead of 0.0.0.0

* fix(grpc): startup parity, cancel interrupt, and cli text fallback

- Replace enableConfigs() with await init() in start-grpc.ts for full
  bootstrap parity with the main CLI (env vars, CA certs, mTLS, proxy,
  OAuth, Windows shell)
- Call engine.interrupt() before call.end() in the cancel handler so
  in-flight model/tool execution is actually stopped
- Show done.full_text in the CLI client when no text_chunk was received,
  preventing silent drops when streaming is unavailable

* fix(grpc): wire session_id end-to-end and remove dead provider field

- Move session_id from ClientMessage into ChatRequest to fix proto-loader
  oneofs encoding bug and make the field functional
- Implement in-memory session store so reconnecting with the same
  session_id resumes conversation context across streams
- Remove ChatRequest.provider — per-request provider routing requires
  global process.env mutation, unsafe for concurrent clients; provider
  is configured via env vars at server startup

* fix(grpc): mirror CLI auth bootstrap in start-grpc and fix tool_name field

scripts/start-grpc.ts now runs the same provider/auth bootstrap as the
normal CLI entrypoint: enableConfigs, safe env vars, Gemini/GitHub token
hydration, saved-profile resolution with warn-and-fallback, and provider
validation before the server binds.

ToolCallResult.tool_name was being populated with the tool_use_id UUID.
Added a toolNameById map (filled in canUseTool) so tool_name now carries
the actual tool name (e.g. "Bash"). The UUID moves to a new tool_use_id
field (proto field 4) for client-side correlation.

* fix(grpc): add tool_use_id to ToolCallStart and interrupt engine on stream close

Two blocker-level issues flagged in code review:

- ToolCallStart was missing tool_use_id, making it impossible for clients
  to correlate tool_start events with tool_result when the same tool runs
  multiple times. Added tool_use_id = 3 to the proto message and populated
  it from the toolUseID parameter in canUseTool.

- On stream close without an explicit CancelSignal the server only nulled
  the engine reference, leaving the underlying model/tool work running
  as an orphan. Added engine.interrupt() in the call.on('end') handler
  to stop work immediately when the client disconnects.

* fix(grpc): resolve pending promises on disconnect and guard post-cancel writes

Four lifecycle and contract issues identified during proactive review:

- Pending permission Promises in canUseTool would hang forever if the
  client disconnected mid-stream. On call 'end', all pending resolvers
  are now called with 'no' so the engine can unblock and terminate.

- The done message and session save could fire after call.end() when
  a CancelSignal arrived mid-generation. Added an `interrupted` flag
  set on both cancel and stream close to gate all post-loop writes.

- The session map had no eviction policy, allowing unbounded memory
  growth. Capped at MAX_SESSIONS=1000 with FIFO eviction of the
  oldest entry.

- Field 3 was silently absent from ChatRequest. Added `reserved 3`
  to document the gap and prevent accidental reuse in future.

* fix(grpc): reset previousMessages on each new request to prevent session history leak

previousMessages was declared at stream scope and only overwritten when
the incoming session_id already existed in the session store. A second
request on the same stream with a new session_id would silently inherit
the first request's conversation history in initialMessages instead of
starting fresh, violating the session contract.

Fix: reset previousMessages to [] at the start of each ChatRequest
before the session-store lookup.

* fix(grpc): reset interrupted flag between requests and guard against concurrent ChatRequest

Two stream-scoped state bugs found during proactive audit:

- The `interrupted` flag was never reset between requests on the same
  stream. If the first request was cancelled, all subsequent requests
  would silently skip the done message, causing the client to hang.

- A second ChatRequest arriving while the first was still processing
  would overwrite the engine reference, corrupting the lifecycle of
  both requests. Now returns ALREADY_EXISTS error instead. Engine is
  nulled after the for-await loop completes so subsequent requests
  can proceed normally.

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>" class="Link--secondary" href="/Gitlawb/openclaude/commit/26eef92fe72e9c3958d61435b8d3571e12bf2b74">feat: add headless gRPC server for external agent integration (</a><a class="issue-link js-issue-link" data-error-text="Failed to load title" data-id="4200935893" data-permission-text="Title is private" data-url="https://github.com/Gitlawb/openclaude/issues/278" data-hovercard-type="pull_request" data-hovercard-url="/Gitlawb/openclaude/pull/278/hovercard" href="https://github.com/Gitlawb/openclaude/pull/278" aria-keyshortcuts="Alt+ArrowUp">#278</a><a data-pjax="true" title="feat: add headless gRPC server for external agent integration (#278)

class="react-directory-row-commit-cell"><div><div class="react-directory-commit-message"><a data-pjax="true" title="feat: add headless gRPC server for external agent integration (#278)

* gRPC Server

* gRPC fix

* UpdProto

* fix: address PR review feedback for gRPC server

- Update bun.lock for new dependencies (frozen-lockfile CI fix)
- Add multi-turn session persistence via initialMessages
- Replace hardcoded done payload with real token counts
- Default bind to localhost instead of 0.0.0.0

* fix(grpc): startup parity, cancel interrupt, and cli text fallback

- Replace enableConfigs() with await init() in start-grpc.ts for full
  bootstrap parity with the main CLI (env vars, CA certs, mTLS, proxy,
  OAuth, Windows shell)
- Call engine.interrupt() before call.end() in the cancel handler so
  in-flight model/tool execution is actually stopped
- Show done.full_text in the CLI client when no text_chunk was received,
  preventing silent drops when streaming is unavailable

* fix(grpc): wire session_id end-to-end and remove dead provider field

- Move session_id from ClientMessage into ChatRequest to fix proto-loader
  oneofs encoding bug and make the field functional
- Implement in-memory session store so reconnecting with the same
  session_id resumes conversation context across streams
- Remove ChatRequest.provider — per-request provider routing requires
  global process.env mutation, unsafe for concurrent clients; provider
  is configured via env vars at server startup

* fix(grpc): mirror CLI auth bootstrap in start-grpc and fix tool_name field

scripts/start-grpc.ts now runs the same provider/auth bootstrap as the
normal CLI entrypoint: enableConfigs, safe env vars, Gemini/GitHub token
hydration, saved-profile resolution with warn-and-fallback, and provider
validation before the server binds.

ToolCallResult.tool_name was being populated with the tool_use_id UUID.
Added a toolNameById map (filled in canUseTool) so tool_name now carries
the actual tool name (e.g. "Bash"). The UUID moves to a new tool_use_id
field (proto field 4) for client-side correlation.

* fix(grpc): add tool_use_id to ToolCallStart and interrupt engine on stream close

Two blocker-level issues flagged in code review:

- ToolCallStart was missing tool_use_id, making it impossible for clients
  to correlate tool_start events with tool_result when the same tool runs
  multiple times. Added tool_use_id = 3 to the proto message and populated
  it from the toolUseID parameter in canUseTool.

- On stream close without an explicit CancelSignal the server only nulled
  the engine reference, leaving the underlying model/tool work running
  as an orphan. Added engine.interrupt() in the call.on('end') handler
  to stop work immediately when the client disconnects.

* fix(grpc): resolve pending promises on disconnect and guard post-cancel writes

Four lifecycle and contract issues identified during proactive review:

- Pending permission Promises in canUseTool would hang forever if the
  client disconnected mid-stream. On call 'end', all pending resolvers
  are now called with 'no' so the engine can unblock and terminate.

- The done message and session save could fire after call.end() when
  a CancelSignal arrived mid-generation. Added an `interrupted` flag
  set on both cancel and stream close to gate all post-loop writes.

- The session map had no eviction policy, allowing unbounded memory
  growth. Capped at MAX_SESSIONS=1000 with FIFO eviction of the
  oldest entry.

- Field 3 was silently absent from ChatRequest. Added `reserved 3`
  to document the gap and prevent accidental reuse in future.

* fix(grpc): reset previousMessages on each new request to prevent session history leak

previousMessages was declared at stream scope and only overwritten when
the incoming session_id already existed in the session store. A second
request on the same stream with a new session_id would silently inherit
the first request's conversation history in initialMessages instead of
starting fresh, violating the session contract.

Fix: reset previousMessages to [] at the start of each ChatRequest
before the session-store lookup.

* fix(grpc): reset interrupted flag between requests and guard against concurrent ChatRequest

Two stream-scoped state bugs found during proactive audit:

- The `interrupted` flag was never reset between requests on the same
  stream. If the first request was cancelled, all subsequent requests
  would silently skip the done message, causing the client to hang.

- A second ChatRequest arriving while the first was still processing
  would overwrite the engine reference, corrupting the lifecycle of
  both requests. Now returns ALREADY_EXISTS error instead. Engine is
  nulled after the for-await loop completes so subsequent requests
  can proceed normally.

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>" class="Link--secondary" href="/Gitlawb/openclaude/commit/26eef92fe72e9c3958d61435b8d3571e12bf2b74">feat: add headless gRPC server for external agent integration (</a><a class="issue-link js-issue-link" data-error-text="Failed to load title" data-id="4200935893" data-permission-text="Title is private" data-url="https://github.com/Gitlawb/openclaude/issues/278" data-hovercard-type="pull_request" data-hovercard-url="/Gitlawb/openclaude/pull/278/hovercard" href="https://github.com/Gitlawb/openclaude/pull/278" aria-keyshortcuts="Alt+ArrowUp">#278</a><a data-pjax="true" title="feat: add headless gRPC server for external agent integration (#278)

class="react-directory-filename-cell"><div class="react-directory-truncate"><a title="bun.lock" aria-label="bun.lock, (File)" class="Link--primary" href="/Gitlawb/openclaude/blob/main/bun.lock" data-discover="true">bun.lock</a></div></div></div></div></td><td class="react-directory-row-commit-cell"><div><div class="react-directory-commit-message"><a data-pjax="true" title="feat: add headless gRPC server for external agent integration (#278)

* gRPC Server

* gRPC fix

* UpdProto

* fix: address PR review feedback for gRPC server

- Update bun.lock for new dependencies (frozen-lockfile CI fix)
- Add multi-turn session persistence via initialMessages
- Replace hardcoded done payload with real token counts
- Default bind to localhost instead of 0.0.0.0

* fix(grpc): startup parity, cancel interrupt, and cli text fallback

- Replace enableConfigs() with await init() in start-grpc.ts for full
  bootstrap parity with the main CLI (env vars, CA certs, mTLS, proxy,
  OAuth, Windows shell)
- Call engine.interrupt() before call.end() in the cancel handler so
  in-flight model/tool execution is actually stopped
- Show done.full_text in the CLI client when no text_chunk was received,
  preventing silent drops when streaming is unavailable

* fix(grpc): wire session_id end-to-end and remove dead provider field

- Move session_id from ClientMessage into ChatRequest to fix proto-loader
  oneofs encoding bug and make the field functional
- Implement in-memory session store so reconnecting with the same
  session_id resumes conversation context across streams
- Remove ChatRequest.provider — per-request provider routing requires
  global process.env mutation, unsafe for concurrent clients; provider
  is configured via env vars at server startup

* fix(grpc): mirror CLI auth bootstrap in start-grpc and fix tool_name field

scripts/start-grpc.ts now runs the same provider/auth bootstrap as the
normal CLI entrypoint: enableConfigs, safe env vars, Gemini/GitHub token
hydration, saved-profile resolution with warn-and-fallback, and provider
validation before the server binds.

ToolCallResult.tool_name was being populated with the tool_use_id UUID.
Added a toolNameById map (filled in canUseTool) so tool_name now carries
the actual tool name (e.g. "Bash"). The UUID moves to a new tool_use_id
field (proto field 4) for client-side correlation.

* fix(grpc): add tool_use_id to ToolCallStart and interrupt engine on stream close

Two blocker-level issues flagged in code review:

- ToolCallStart was missing tool_use_id, making it impossible for clients
  to correlate tool_start events with tool_result when the same tool runs
  multiple times. Added tool_use_id = 3 to the proto message and populated
  it from the toolUseID parameter in canUseTool.

- On stream close without an explicit CancelSignal the server only nulled
  the engine reference, leaving the underlying model/tool work running
  as an orphan. Added engine.interrupt() in the call.on('end') handler
  to stop work immediately when the client disconnects.

* fix(grpc): resolve pending promises on disconnect and guard post-cancel writes

Four lifecycle and contract issues identified during proactive review:

- Pending permission Promises in canUseTool would hang forever if the
  client disconnected mid-stream. On call 'end', all pending resolvers
  are now called with 'no' so the engine can unblock and terminate.

- The done message and session save could fire after call.end() when
  a CancelSignal arrived mid-generation. Added an `interrupted` flag
  set on both cancel and stream close to gate all post-loop writes.

- The session map had no eviction policy, allowing unbounded memory
  growth. Capped at MAX_SESSIONS=1000 with FIFO eviction of the
  oldest entry.

- Field 3 was silently absent from ChatRequest. Added `reserved 3`
  to document the gap and prevent accidental reuse in future.

* fix(grpc): reset previousMessages on each new request to prevent session history leak

previousMessages was declared at stream scope and only overwritten when
the incoming session_id already existed in the session store. A second
request on the same stream with a new session_id would silently inherit
the first request's conversation history in initialMessages instead of
starting fresh, violating the session contract.

Fix: reset previousMessages to [] at the start of each ChatRequest
before the session-store lookup.

* fix(grpc): reset interrupted flag between requests and guard against concurrent ChatRequest

Two stream-scoped state bugs found during proactive audit:

- The `interrupted` flag was never reset between requests on the same
  stream. If the first request was cancelled, all subsequent requests
  would silently skip the done message, causing the client to hang.

- A second ChatRequest arriving while the first was still processing
  would overwrite the engine reference, corrupting the lifecycle of
  both requests. Now returns ALREADY_EXISTS error instead. Engine is
  nulled after the for-await loop completes so subsequent requests
  can proceed normally.

---------

before the session-store lookup.

* fix(grpc): reset interrupted flag between requests and guard against concurrent ChatRequest

Two stream-scoped state bugs found during proactive audit:

- The `interrupted` flag was never reset between requests on the same
  stream. If the first request was cancelled, all subsequent requests
  would silently skip the done message, causing the client to hang.

- A second ChatRequest arriving while the first was still processing
  would overwrite the engine reference, corrupting the lifecycle of
  both requests. Now returns ALREADY_EXISTS error instead. Engine is
  nulled after the for-await loop completes so subsequent requests
  can proceed normally.

---------

<p dir="auto">OpenClaude is an open-source coding-agent CLI for cloud and local model providers.</p>
<p dir="auto">Use OpenAI-compatible APIs, Gemini, GitHub Models, Codex, Ollama, Atomic Chat, and other supported backends while keeping one terminal-first workflow: prompts, tools, agents, MCP, slash commands, and streaming output.</p>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Why OpenClaude</h2><a id="user-content-why-openclaude" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Why OpenClaude" href="#why-openclaude"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Use one CLI across cloud APIs and local model backends</li>
<li>Save provider profiles inside the app with <code>/provider</code></li>
<li>Run with OpenAI-compatible services, Gemini, GitHub Models, Codex, Ollama, Atomic Chat, and other supported providers</li>
<li>Keep coding-agent workflows in one place: bash, file tools, grep, glob, agents, tasks, MCP, and web tools</li>
<li>Use the bundled VS Code extension for launch integration and theme support</li>
</ul>

<p dir="auto">Inside OpenClaude:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>run <code>/provider</code> for guided provider setup and saved profiles</li>
<li>run <code>/onboard-github</code> for GitHub Models onboarding</li>
</ul>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Supported Providers</h2><a id="user-content-supported-providers" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Supported Providers" href="#supported-providers"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">What Works</h2><a id="user-content-what-works" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: What Works" href="#what-works"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Tool-driven coding workflows</strong>: Bash, file read/write/edit, grep, glob, agents, tasks, MCP, and slash commands</li>
<li><strong>Streaming responses</strong>: Real-time token output and tool progress</li>
<li><strong>Tool calling</strong>: Multi-step tool loops with model calls, tool execution, and follow-up responses</li>
<li><strong>Images</strong>: URL and base64 image inputs for providers that support vision</li>
<li><strong>Provider profiles</strong>: Guided setup plus saved <code>.openclaude-profile.json</code> support</li>
<li><strong>Local and remote model backends</strong>: Cloud APIs, local servers, and Apple Silicon local inference</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Provider Notes</h2><a id="user-content-provider-notes" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Provider Notes" href="#provider-notes"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">OpenClaude supports multiple providers, but behavior is not identical across all of them.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Anthropic-specific features may not exist on other providers</li>
<li>Tool quality depends heavily on the selected model</li>
<li>Smaller local models can struggle with long multi-step tool flows</li>
<li>Some providers impose lower output caps than the CLI defaults, and OpenClaude adapts where possible</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">For best results, use models with strong tool/function calling support.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Agent Routing</h2><a id="user-content-agent-routing" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Agent Routing" href="#agent-routing"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">OpenClaude can route different agents to different models through settings-based routing. This is useful for cost optimization or splitting work by model strength.</p>
<p dir="auto">Add to <code>~/.claude/settings.json</code>:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-json notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>{
  <span class="pl-ent">"agentModels"</span>: {
    <span class="pl-ent">"deepseek-chat"</span>: {
      <span class="pl-ent">"base_url"</span>: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>https://api.deepseek.com/v1<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>,
      <span class="pl-ent">"api_key"</span>: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>sk-your-key<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>
    },
    <span class="pl-ent">"gpt-4o"</span>: {
      <span class="pl-ent">"base_url"</span>: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>https://api.openai.com/v1<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>,
      <span class="pl-ent">"api_key"</span>: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>sk-your-key<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>
    }
  },
  <span class="pl-ent">"agentRouting"</span>: {
    <span class="pl-ent">"Explore"</span>: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>deepseek-chat<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>,
    <span class="pl-ent">"Plan"</span>: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>gpt-4o<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>,
    <span class="pl-ent">"general-purpose"</span>: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>gpt-4o<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>,
    <span class="pl-ent">"frontend-dev"</span>: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>deepseek-chat<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>,
    <span class="pl-ent">"default"</span>: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>gpt-4o<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>
  }
}</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="{
  "agentModels": {
    "deepseek-chat": {
      "base_url": "https://api.deepseek.com/v1",
      "api_key": "sk-your-key"
    },
    "gpt-4o": {
      "base_url": "https://api.openai.com/v1",
      "api_key": "sk-your-key"
    }
  },
  "agentRouting": {
    "Explore": "deepseek-chat",
    "Plan": "gpt-4o",
    "general-purpose": "gpt-4o",

    "default": "gpt-4o"
  }
}" tabindex="0" role="button">
      <svg aria-hidden="true" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" data-view-component="true" class="octicon octicon-copy js-clipboard-copy-icon">

<p dir="auto">When no routing match is found, the global provider remains the fallback.</p>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Note:</strong> <code>api_key</code> values in <code>settings.json</code> are stored in plaintext. Keep this file private and do not commit it to version control.</p>
</blockquote>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Web Search and Fetch</h2><a id="user-content-web-search-and-fetch" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Web Search and Fetch" href="#web-search-and-fetch"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">By default, <code>WebSearch</code> works on non-Anthropic models using DuckDuckGo. This gives GPT-4o, DeepSeek, Gemini, Ollama, and other OpenAI-compatible providers a free web search path out of the box.</p>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Note:</strong> DuckDuckGo fallback works by scraping search results and may be rate-limited, blocked, or subject to DuckDuckGo's Terms of Service. If you want a more reliable supported option, configure Firecrawl.</p>
</blockquote>
<p dir="auto">For Anthropic-native backends and Codex responses, OpenClaude keeps the native provider web search behavior.</p>
<p dir="auto"><code>WebFetch</code> works, but its basic HTTP plus HTML-to-markdown path can still fail on JavaScript-rendered sites or sites that block plain HTTP requests.</p>
<p dir="auto">Set a <a href="https://firecrawl.dev" rel="nofollow">Firecrawl</a> API key if you want Firecrawl-powered search/fetch behavior:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-k">export</span> FIRECRAWL_API_KEY=your-key-here</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="export FIRECRAWL_API_KEY=your-key-here" tabindex="0" role="button">
      <svg aria-hidden="true" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" data-view-component="true" class="octicon octicon-copy js-clipboard-copy-icon">
    <path d="M0 6.75C0 5.784.784 5 1.75 5h1.5a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.5h-1.5a.25.25 0 0 0-.25.25v7.5c0 .138.112.25.25.25h7.5a.25.25 0 0 0 .25-.25v-1.5a.75.75 0 0 1 1.5 0v1.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 9.25 16h-7.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 0 14.25Z"></path><path d="M5 1.75C5 .784 5.784 0 6.75 0h7.5C15.216 0 16 .784 16 1.75v7.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 14.25 11h-7.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 5 9.25Zm1.75-.25a.25.25 0 0 0-.25.25v7.5c0 .138.112.25.25.25h7.5a.25.25 0 0 0 .25-.25v-7.5a.25.25 0 0 0-.25-.25Z"></path>
</svg>
      <svg aria-hidden="true" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" data-view-component="true" class="octicon octicon-check js-clipboard-check-icon color-fg-success d-none">
    <path d="M13.78 4.22a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.06l-7.25 7.25a.75.75 0 0 1-1.06 0L2.22 9.28a.751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018L6 10.94l6.72-6.72a.75.75 0 0 1 1.06 0Z"></path>
</svg>
    </clipboard-copy>
  </div></div>
<p dir="auto">With Firecrawl enabled:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>WebSearch</code> can use Firecrawl's search API while DuckDuckGo remains the default free path for non-Claude models</li>
<li><code>WebFetch</code> uses Firecrawl's scrape endpoint instead of raw HTTP, handling JS-rendered pages correctly</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Free tier at <a href="https://firecrawl.dev" rel="nofollow">firecrawl.dev</a> includes 500 credits. The key is optional.</p>
<hr>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Headless gRPC Server</h2><a id="user-content-headless-grpc-server" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Headless gRPC Server" href="#headless-grpc-server"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">OpenClaude can be run as a headless gRPC service, allowing you to integrate its agentic capabilities (tools, bash, file editing) into other applications, CI/CD pipelines, or custom user interfaces. The server uses bidirectional streaming to send real-time text chunks, tool calls, and request permissions for sensitive commands.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">1. Start the gRPC Server</h3><a id="user-content-1-start-the-grpc-server" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 1. Start the gRPC Server" href="#1-start-the-grpc-server"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Start the core engine as a gRPC service on <code>localhost:50051</code>:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>npm run dev:grpc</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="npm run dev:grpc" tabindex="0" role="button">
      <svg aria-hidden="true" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" data-view-component="true" class="octicon octicon-copy js-clipboard-copy-icon">

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Configuration</h4><a id="user-content-configuration" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Configuration" href="#configuration"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<markdown-accessiblity-table data-catalyst=""><table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Variable</th>
<th>Default</th>
<th>Description</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>GRPC_PORT</code></td>
<td><code>50051</code></td>
<td>Port the gRPC server listens on</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>GRPC_HOST</code></td>
<td><code>localhost</code></td>
<td>Bind address. Use <code>0.0.0.0</code> to expose on all interfaces (not recommended without authentication)</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></markdown-accessiblity-table>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">2. Run the Test CLI Client</h3><a id="user-content-2-run-the-test-cli-client" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 2. Run the Test CLI Client" href="#2-run-the-test-cli-client"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">We provide a lightweight CLI client that communicates exclusively over gRPC. It acts just like the main interactive CLI, rendering colors, streaming tokens, and prompting you for tool permissions (y/n) via the gRPC <code>action_required</code> event.</p>
<p dir="auto">In a separate terminal, run:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>npm run dev:grpc:cli</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="npm run dev:grpc:cli" tabindex="0" role="button">
      <svg aria-hidden="true" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" data-view-component="true" class="octicon octicon-copy js-clipboard-copy-icon">

<p dir="auto"><em>Note: The gRPC definitions are located in <code>src/proto/openclaude.proto</code>. You can use this file to generate clients in Python, Go, Rust, or any other language.</em></p>
<hr>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Testing And Coverage</h2><a id="user-content-testing-and-coverage" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Testing And Coverage" href="#testing-and-coverage"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>

<p dir="auto">Use focused test runs when you only touch one area:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>bun run test:provider</code></li>
<li><code>bun run test:provider-recommendation</code></li>
<li><code>bun test path/to/file.test.ts</code></li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Recommended contributor validation before opening a PR:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>bun run build</code></li>
<li><code>bun run smoke</code></li>
<li><code>bun run test:coverage</code> for broader unit coverage when your change affects shared runtime or provider logic</li>
<li>focused <code>bun test ...</code> runs for the files and flows you changed</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Coverage output is written to <code>coverage/lcov.info</code>, and OpenClaude also generates a git-activity-style heatmap at <code>coverage/index.html</code>.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Repository Structure</h2><a id="user-content-repository-structure" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Repository Structure" href="#repository-structure"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>src/</code> - core CLI/runtime</li>
<li><code>scripts/</code> - build, verification, and maintenance scripts</li>
<li><code>docs/</code> - setup, contributor, and project documentation</li>
<li><code>python/</code> - standalone Python helpers and their tests</li>
<li><code>vscode-extension/openclaude-vscode/</code> - VS Code extension</li>
<li><code>.github/</code> - repo automation, templates, and CI configuration</li>
<li><code>bin/</code> - CLI launcher entrypoints</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">VS Code Extension</h2><a id="user-content-vs-code-extension" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: VS Code Extension" href="#vs-code-extension"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">The repo includes a VS Code extension in <a href="/Gitlawb/openclaude/blob/main/vscode-extension/openclaude-vscode"><code>vscode-extension/openclaude-vscode</code></a> for OpenClaude launch integration, provider-aware control-center UI, and theme support.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Security</h2><a id="user-content-security" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Security" href="#security"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">If you believe you found a security issue, see <a href="/Gitlawb/openclaude/blob/main/SECURITY.md">SECURITY.md</a>.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Community</h2><a id="user-content-community" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Community" href="#community"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Use <a href="https://github.com/Gitlawb/openclaude/discussions">GitHub Discussions</a> for Q&A, ideas, and community conversation</li>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Contributing</h2><a id="user-content-contributing" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Contributing" href="#contributing"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Contributions are welcome.</p>
<p dir="auto">For larger changes, open an issue first so the scope is clear before implementation. Helpful validation commands include:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>bun run build</code></li>
<li><code>bun run test:coverage</code></li>
<li><code>bun run smoke</code></li>
<li>focused <code>bun test ...</code> runs for touched areas</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Disclaimer</h2><a id="user-content-disclaimer" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Disclaimer" href="#disclaimer"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
